prompt: I start a TCP listener in an AnyIO task group and then sleep for 0.1 seconds before connecting, because that usually gives it time to bind. Sometimes CI flakes. One code path even forgets to call `task_status.started()` after binding. Show the correct readiness pattern and tell me the exact failure behavior that `start()` gives me when the child never signals readiness.
assertions: It recommends `TaskGroup.start()` instead of a guessed sleep for readiness.
It states that the started task must call `task_status.started()`.
It mentions that `TaskGroup.start()` raises `RuntimeError` if the child never signals readiness.
It explains that the sleep-based pattern is hiding a startup handshake bug rather than solving it.
It explains that `start()` surfaces startup failures or bind errors immediately instead of turning them into later connection races.
It keeps the answer in AnyIO structured-concurrency terms instead of drifting into generic retry advice.
